items.title: Boy, 16, killed, two others injured in Wadeye quad bike crash

children.content: A 16-year-old boy has been killed in a quad bike crash that also injured a 12-year-old boy and a 19-year-old man in Wadeye on Wednesday.

image.altText: Close up image of an NT Police sign, behind barbed wire fence, with left of frame slightly obstructed by palm tree leaves.

items.title: 101yo Bombing of Darwin veteran wants Australians to know what unfolded

children.content: At 101 years old, Bombing of Darwin survivor Terrance Byrne wants Australians to know exactly what unfolded during the World War II attack on Darwin — still the single largest foreign attack on Australian soil.

image.altText: An older man sitting in front of table of framed black and white pictures.

items.title: Meth, money, menaces: Kingpin's private phone calls expose bikie secrets

children.content: Detectives say bikies are recruiting inside Northern Territory prisons, as a kingpin's tapped private phone calls offer a rare glimpse into the underground world of outlaw motorcycle gangs.

gallery.altText: A red white and blue skull embroidered into patches sewn into vests.

thumbnail.altText: A red white and blue skull embroidered into patches sewn into vests.

items.title: Housing plan aims to attract more students to international 'gateway' city

children.content: A towering 14-storey addition to Darwin's skyline is expected to ease housing stress for university students, with $70 million invested to tackle the city's competitive rental market.

image.altText: A sketch of a multi-storey building based in the city, the CDU logo can be seen on the side of the accommodation tower.

items.title: New $30.5m funding package to help repair NT roads damaged by flooding

children.content: The Northern Territory government has announced $30.5 million in funding to repair roads damaged by wet season flooding across the territory.

image.altText: A remote road collapsed to one side.

items.title: Officer who disarmed woman in DV shelter tells of 'worst day' of her career

children.content: An inquest hears evidence from a senior police officer who ran into a room to disarm a mother who was self-harming inside a Darwin crisis accommodation facility, describing the incident as "one of the worst days" of her policing career.

articles.title: Paramedics unable to help bleeding mother for almost an hour, inquest hears

children.content: A paramedic tells an inquest she decided to wait for police back-up before attending to a woman who was actively harming herself in a Northern Territory domestic violence shelter.

image.altText: The exterior of the Darwin Local Court.

gallery.altText: A police car outside the Darwin Local Court.

thumbnail.altText: A police car outside the Darwin Local Court.

avatar.alt: A journalist wears a pattern blouse and serious facial expression.

items.title: Remote residents 'cooking from the inside' sue NT government

children.content: Residents of the Central Australian community of Papunya say the scorching hot outback summers are killing their people in unsafe housing. Now they’re suing the Northern Territory government to try to force change.

image.altText: A drone shot of a small remote community, which features a small number of homes built on the red dirt landscape.

items.title: 'How many beers have you had?' Flood evacuees claim racial profiling

children.content: Flood evacuees from remote Northern Territory communities say they are being "treated like prisoners" by security guards working at their emergency accommodation centre.

image.altText: Signs attached to black fencing reads: no alcohol, no visitors

images.altText: Three ships and a small dinghy on a body of water.
Large container ship on waterways in the Strait of Hormuz

image.altText: Masked men in sand-coloured army fatigues climbing a ladder off the side of a ship.

children.content: Iran has said it will not reopen the Strait of Hormuz as long as the US naval blockade remains in place. Follow live.

items.title: Iran attacks three cargo ships in strait, US Navy Secretary departs role

updates.title: Iranian state media shows video of soldiers boarding container ships
US secretary of the navy departing the administration
US CENTCOM denies reports of Iran-linked ships evading blockade

items.title: New AI threat looms but Australian firms don't have access needed to prepare

children.content: Local banks, power providers and infrastructure firms do not have access to test their systems against a powerful new AI cybersecurity risk, Anthropic's Mythos, which has not been made public.

image.altText: Anthropic logo on a smartphone screen

items.title: Pacific leaders 'totally blindsided' by declaration of fuel emergency

children.content: Confusion and some frustration remains after a key Pacific leader announced an emergency declaration had been agreed to over fuel, despite other leaders not knowing about it.

image.altText: A man in a suit rests his hand on his chest, standing in front of Solomon Island and China flags.

items.title: Police officer given 'golden pass' on rape allegations, victim says

children.content: When Tasmania Police misidentified Laura as a family violence perpetrator, she had no idea she'd have to fight for them to hold her abuser — a serving officer — to account. Now, she's fighting for change.

gallery.altText: A police officer's torso showing a body cam

thumbnail.altText: A police officer's torso showing a body cam

items.title: No more green bin pick-ups for some residents amid fuel uncertainty

children.content: The Upper Lachlan Shire Council says it cannot afford to be caught off-guard if a diesel shipment does not arrive, and must reserve fuel for essential services like road grading or red bin collections.

image.altText: Council bins with green lids on suburban street.

items.title: Major ASX healthcare stock plunge continues

children.content: Major healthcare stock Cochlear crashed yesterday. Buyers have not moved in today. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: Farmers say an easy solution to the mouse plague has been rejected

children.content: Farmers are pushing for stronger bait approval as mice numbers soar out of control in Western Australia, but stronger poisons
are being rejected by the national chemical regulator.

image.altText: Feral mice in a bucket.

items.title: Thousands sign petition against McDonald's Phillip Island restaurant plan

children.content: The fast food giant is facing backlash after lodging a planning permit application to build a new restaurant in tourist hotspot Cowes.

image.altText: Artist render of a McDonald's restaurant.

items.title: 'Not a great look': Piastri weighs in on Verstappen's F1 frustrations

children.content: Australian star Oscar Piastri speaks as Formula 1 champion Max Verstappen looks increasingly likely to walk away from the sport.

gallery.altText: An image of three F1 drivers speaking during an interview in pit lane.

thumbnail.altText: An image of three F1 drivers speaking during an interview in pit lane.

items.title: Stump that created hole in footpath becomes neighbourhood attraction

children.content: Adelaide residents have decorated a large tree stump and then the hole it left in a footpath to highlight the local council's delay in removing it.

image.altText: A rotten tree stump surrounded by traffic cones, including one topped by a green hat

items.title: Small WA town takes centre stage for Netflix series filming

children.content: There is a buzz about town in a small community north of Perth as filming begins for a new Netflix series.

image.altText: A film crew in front of beige buildings.

items.title: This man plays the last post to a different fallen Anzac every day

children.content: Adam Farquharson feels the honour of Anzac Day every day as he plays to a different veteran's grave.

gallery.altText: A man in black plays a silver instrument in a graveyard

thumbnail.altText: A man in black plays a silver instrument in a graveyard

items.title: Alleged drink driver detected at 151km/h with child in ute, police say

children.content: A man who was allegedly drink driving with a child inside a speeding ute in Adelaide's south last night will face court after being stripped of his licence, police say.

image.altText: An SA Police logo.

items.title: 70yo 'duped' into importing heroin to Sydney in fold-out camping bed jailed

children.content: Barry James Calverley's lawyers argued he was an innocent drug mule who naively and gullibly trusted a story about why he needed to bring the bed to Australia.

image.altText: Barry Calverley interviewed by police.

items.title: 'Additional character' in payroll file leaves hospital staff unpaid

children.content: Health Minister Rachel Stephen-Smith says the government is working to resolve the issue that has left thousands of North Canberra Hospital staff waiting for their fortnightly pay.

image.altText: North Canberra Hospital exterior

items.title: 15yo boy dies in collision involving e-motorbike in Melbourne's north

children.content: A teenager died after being thrown from an unregistered electric motorcycle at South Morang on Wednesday evening.

image.altText: Two police officers standing near a bike.

items.title: Five per cent home deposit scheme fuelling price hike, report suggests

children.content: New research from property analytics firm Cotality appears to show that the Home Guarantee Scheme, intended to help first home buyers, may be worsening entry level affordability.

gallery.altText: A house with a sold sign in the front yard.

thumbnail.altText: A house with a sold sign in the front yard.

items.title: Removing plastic from our food chain yields quick results, researchers say

children.content: Perth researchers find levels of plastic chemicals in the body can be reduced within a week by avoiding plastic food packaging and kitchen utensils.

image.altText: Bottles, tin, glass

items.title: Ex-boxer and convicted kidnapper fighting for life after Sydney shooting

children.content: A former champion boxer is fighting for life after a suspected gangland hit was one of two violent incidents in Sydney's west overnight.

image.altText: Humzah Khalid

items.title: 50 years of Ramones — the album nobody bought but everyone copied

children.content: Fed up with where rock 'n' roll was headed, these four New Yorkers changed the music world by playing sweet pop hooks at breakneck speed.

gallery.altText: Four members of Ramones stand outdoors in 1970s-style jeans and graphic T‑shirts, posing in front of flowering bushes

thumbnail.altText: Four members of Ramones stand outdoors in 1970s-style jeans and graphic T‑shirts, posing in front of flowering bushes

items.title: Landholders band together against coal seam gas development

children.content: The Toprain Farming Collective is calling for greater protection of strategic cropping land in central Queensland, as Comet Ridge proposes an expansion and further development of the Mahalo Gas Hub.

image.altText: Farmer Ruth Jones frowns, appearing unimpressed. Behind her is sign labeled 'danger', and a grey building in a grassy field.

items.title: Blockchain billionaire takes Trump family's crypto firm to court

children.content: A lawsuit alleges that World Liberty secretly installed tools to prevent the sale of Justin Sun's tokens after they became tradable in September 2025.

image.altText: a man holds a microphone to his mouth while delivering a speech at a crypto event

items.title: Australian billionaire's waste-to-energy plan labelled 'waste colonialism'

children.content: The controversial project would see waste burned to create energy in Fiji to reduce the country's reliance on fossil fuels, but locals and environmentalists are not happy.

image.altText: A deep-water port with large cranes, a cargo ship and shipping containers sits in front of a large building with two chimneys

items.title: Aussies crash in Madrid as Kasatkina sinks to new ranking low

children.content: Daria Kasatkina's difficult WTA Tour season has continued with an agonising defeat at the Madrid Open, as all four Aussie women crash out in the opening round.

image.altText: Daria Kasatkina gets ready to play a shot
